articles.title: India: Growth and inflation risks from energy shock – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Michael Wan highlight that a Strait of Hormuz closure would hit India through Oil, Liquified Petroleum Gas and Natural Gas Liquids shortages, with spillovers to fertiliser, food production and inflation.

articles.title: The USD is mixed as the market prepares for the North American session
articles.expand: The USD is trading in a mixed and mostly range-bound fashion to start the North American session. The dollar is firmer against the EUR and GBP, with EURUSD and GBPUSD edging lower, while the greenback is softer against the JPY after pushing toward 2026 highs earlier and finding willing sellers on the test. In the video above, I break down the three...

articles.title: AUD: RBA hike bets and 0.7200 target – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole highlights the Australian Dollar as a key G10 outperformer, supported by strong RBA hike expectations and high Oil prices. Markets now assign a 70% probability to a 25bp hike next week, with AUD/USD seen targeting 0.7200 if equities hold up.

articles.title: Wall Street Signals Negative Start
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - Ongoing hostilities in the Middle East have heightened oil and gas price volatility, fueling inflation expectations. Furthermore, the potential shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz is intensifying concerns over significant supply disruptions.

articles.title: GBP/USD at the Crossroads: Will cable break the 1.3437 resistance?
articles.expand: The GBP/USD is stalled at a critical 1.3437 resistance level as the US Dollar gains strength from global conflict safe-haven flows and rising inflation fears. articles.title: India's inflation rate increases to 3.2% in February, slightly above consensus
articles.expand: CPI Y/Y 3.2% vs 3.1% expected Prior 2.1% (revised to 2.7%) India’s retail inflation accelerated more than expected in February, reaching 3.21% on an annual basis. This follows a revised reading of 2.74% in January and surpasses the 3.1% consensus estimate predicted by economists. The latest data, released by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme...

articles.title: India reportedly sees first oil vessel reach its port since the US-Iran conflict started
articles.expand: This is an interesting development to say the least. There are reports going around that an oil tanker has made its way from Saudi Arabia to India via the Strait of Hormuz, the first such case since the US-Iran conflict started. The 'Shenlong Suezmax' is an oil tanker owned by Shenlong Shipping Ltd and managed by Dynacom Tanker Management Ltd, has ...

articles.title: Chart alert: WTI crude oil resumes uptrend above $88.00 despite historical IEA stockpile release
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate crude oil remains highly volatile as the US–Iran war 2026 fuels fears of supply disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz. Prices rebounded despite a record 400-million-barrel emergency release by the International Energy Agency. Technically, WTI is holding above $88.36 support, keeping the short-term bullish trend intact with p...

articles.title: EUR/GBP: Market too hawkish on BoE path – ING
articles.expand: ING analysts see markets as overly aggressive on Bank of England expectations, with easing priced out after the Iran conflict. They note EUR/GBP’s negative correlation with Oil and warn that no rate changes are now expected by year-end.

articles.title: ECB: Hawkish comments drive rate expectations – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Rates Strategist Hauke Siemßen argues that recent Oil-driven moves in Euro rates have given way to ECB-driven repricing. Comments from Kazimir and Schnabel have led forwards to discount a first 25 bp ECB rate hike by July, even as Commerzbank still forecasts no hikes this year.

articles.title: AUD/USD: RBA hawkish stance supports resilience – DBS
articles.expand: Philip Wee at DBS Group Research highlights that the Australian Dollar has outperformed in G10 despite the Iran conflict. He attributes AUD strength to Reserve Bank of Australia policy divergence and a firmly hawkish stance.

articles.title: GBPUSD rejects a major trendline as US dollar bids return amid renewed risk aversion
articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW USD: The US dollar weakened across the board earlier this week as oil prices fell following G7 discussion about emergency oil reserves release. The move accelerated after Trump told CBS that “the war could be over soon.” Traders began unwinding some of their positions, as expectations of a quick resolution led markets to dial b...

articles.title: JPY: Energy shock raises bar for intervention – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lee Hardman notes the Japanese Yen has underperformed since the Middle East conflict, with USD/JPY back near year-to-date highs.

articles.title: Señal Forex EUR/USD Hoy: El Impulso Bajista se Reanuda a Medida que los Precios del Petróleo Suben - 12 marzo 2026
articles.expand: El EUR/USD extiende su retroceso mientras los precios del petróleo suben y aumentan las preocupaciones sobre la economía europea. Los mercados vigilan energía, inflación y próximos datos de EE. UU.

articles.title: ECB: Hawkish words as oil shock risk rises – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Global Head of Macro Carsten Brzeski argues that the European Central Bank will keep rates unchanged at its 19 March meeting but adopt a more hawkish tone as the war in the Middle East and higher Oil prices revive memories of the 2022 energy shock.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Hawkish BOJ risks at multi‑decade highs – DBS
articles.expand: DBS Group Research’s Philip Wee warns that caution is warranted on USD/JPY as the pair trades near the 159–160 resistance zone after Operation Epic Fury. He expects the Bank of Japan to deliver a hawkish hold on March 19, distinguishing temporary supply-led inflation from demand-pull pressures.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Real rate shock weighs on Euro – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong argue that higher nominal ECB rate expectations are failing to support the Euro as Oil-driven stagflation erodes the euro area’s real return and worsens its external balance.

articles.title: Middle East energy disruption shifts EUR/USD risks to the downside - Danske
articles.expand: Before the US-Iran conflict, EUR/USD took a run at the 1.2000 level as upside risks looked more likely to win out as we started the year. But in the last two weeks, the script has completely flipped amid surging energy prices globally. In particular, European gas has also surged higher in following oil prices as Iran threatens further disruption in...

articles.title: ECB: Hawkish voices grow on energy and fiscal risks – Danske Bank
articles.expand: Danske Research Team reports that ECB officials are turning more hawkish as energy-driven inflation risks and potential fiscal easing in the Euro area build.

articles.title: While the World Panics, the Aussie Is Rallying. What’s Up With That?!
articles.expand: The Aussie rally gained traction as hawkish RBA signals, stronger energy exports, and broader U.S. dollar weakness all lined up.

articles.title: Drone strikes hit Oman energy facility, vessels evacuate Mina Al Fahal terminal
articles.expand: Summary: Drone strikes reportedly linked to Iran triggered fires at Oman’s Mina petroleum facility near Salalah. Authorities evacuated all vessels from the Mina Al Fahal oil export terminal as a precaution. The terminal handles roughly 1 million barrels per day of Omani crude exports. Mina Al Fahal is strategically located outside the Strait of Hor...

articles.title: AUD/JPY rally accelerates, GBP/AUD falls as RBA turns more activist on inflation
articles.expand: Aussie’s broad-based strength continues today as markets further strengthened expectations that the RBA will deliver two consecutive rate hikes in the coming months. Investors are increasingly convinced that the RBA will raise the cash rate at the March 17 meeting and follow up with another increase in May, lifting the policy rate from the cu...

articles.title: Reuters says that Global shipper CMA CGM resumes bookings from Gulf ports
articles.expand: Reuters is reporting that CMA CGM has reopened container bookings from major Gulf export hubs, signalling improving conditions for regional shipping after recent disruptions. articles.title: ANZ joins banks expecting March 17 RBA rate hike as oil shock lifts inflation risks
articles.expand: Expectations for an RBA rate hike on March 17 are strengthening as oil-driven inflation risks grow and major banks including ANZ join forecasts for tighter policy. Summary: RBA Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser warned that oil price shocks tied to the Iran conflict pose upside risks to inflation. He said there will be “genuine policy debate” at the Mar...

articles.title: UK housing market cools as RICS price gauge falls to -12
articles.expand: UK housing demand weakened in February as geopolitical tensions and higher energy prices raised fears mortgage rates could remain elevated. articles.title: Australian share market: Oil price gains and tanker attacks drive ASX200 back deep in the red
articles.expand: Investor nerves over just how long the US-Israeli war with Iran will last and growing fears over global oil supply is driving the Australian share market back deep into the red.

articles.title: My flight has been cancelled: What are my rights?
articles.expand: If flights are cancelled because of conflict - for example where war closes an airspace - then airlines are not required to offer passengers a refund. But that doesn’t necessarily cover a surge in fuel prices.

articles.title: Canada will 'do its part' to help IEA release of oil reserves: Hodgson
articles.expand: OTTAWA — Natural Resources Minister Tim Hodgson said Wednesday Canada will "do its part" to lower the cost of oil globally as a bloc of nations looks to tame the surge in energy prices triggered by the war in the Middle East.

articles.expand: Australia Consumer Inflation Expectation for March 2025 rise to 5.2% from 5% in February. articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Focus now shifts to 0.7200
articles.expand: Sticky domestic inflation, together with a Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) that still shows little appetite to soften its hawkish stance, continues to offer a supportive backdrop for the Australian Dollar (AUD).

articles.title: BOJ not expected to raise interest rates next week - poll
articles.expand: The survey sees all 64 economists anticipating that the BOJ will keep its policy rate unchanged at 0.75% next week. Meanwhile, roughly 60% (37 out of 62 economists) see the Japanese central bank making a move to raise the policy rate to 1.00% by end-June. That is not too much changed from the ~58% in the February poll. Of the 44 economists who spec...

articles.expand: This Loonie pair just busted though a major ceiling but appears to be stalling at another resistance level. Can it go for a correction to this area of interest?

articles.expand: S P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq Analysis and Trading Levels: Stock Markets rebound as Oil stops rising, but the path ahead faces clouds. Stocks will need a war resolution before a more consistent rebound.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Bulls look to seize control amid hopes for end to Iran war
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair attracts buyers for the third consecutive day and climbs to over a one-week high, around the 1.3485 region, during the first half of the European session on Tuesday.

articles.title: USDCHF Technicals: USDCHF trapped between 100-hour and 200-hour moving averages
articles.expand: The USDCHF gapped higher at the start of trading, pushing the pair above the 100-hour moving average (blue line on the chart). However, the upside momentum quickly stalled. articles.expand: The GBPUSD fell sharply at the open, extending below a key swing area between 1.3298 and 1.3305. The downside momentum pushed the pair to a session low at 1.3282 before buyers stepped in and sparked a rebound.
